intake.event_framing: Assess rare-earth supply disruption after export restrictions and port delays.
intake.current_development: Export restrictions and port delays disrupt a rare-earth supply corridor for a battery maker.
known_unknowns: How quickly substitute processing routes can be certified

summary: Customers will tolerate short delays before switching suppliers

documents.rare-earth-ports.md: Port delays and export restrictions hit rare-earth materials needed by the battery maker.
documents.rare-earth-substitute.md: Procurement teams scramble to qualify alternate sources for specialized rare-earth processing.

intake.event_framing: Assess an auto supply chain after flooding shuts a single-source electronics plant.
intake.current_development: Severe flooding shuts a single-source electronics plant feeding a major automaker.
known_unknowns: Whether the supplier can restart partial output within two weeks

summary: The automaker will prioritize its highest-margin models

documents.supplier-flood.md: Flooding disrupts a single-source electronics plant and leaves the automaker with thin inventory cover.
documents.supplier-reroute.md: The automaker and logistics partners consider rerouting and alternate suppliers to keep launches alive.

case_title: Francis Scott Key Bridge collapse and Baltimore port closure
historical_outcome: Authorities and shippers first relied on safety restrictions, rerouting, and staged channel restoration to keep freight moving after the bridge collapse blocked Baltimore port access.

sources.title: Port of Baltimore Safety Zone – Francis Scott Key Bridge Marine Casualty
Governor Moore Announces the Full Reopening of the Port of Baltimore

intake.event_framing: Assess East Coast supply-chain responses after the March 2024 Francis Scott Key Bridge collapse closes Baltimore port access.
intake.current_development: The Francis Scott Key Bridge collapse triggered a safety zone, blocked port access, and forced emergency rerouting across freight networks.
known_constraints: Port access is constrained until the channel is cleared
Shippers must preserve deliveries with alternate routing
known_unknowns: How quickly shipping channels can reopen safely

summary: Operators will prioritize rerouting and reserve logistics before attempting larger network redesigns

documents.safety-zone.md: After the Key Bridge collapse, the Coast Guard established a safety zone and vessel movements were restricted around the Port of Baltimore.
documents.port-recovery.md: Maryland later described a coordinated effort to clear the shipping channel and restore port operations after the collapse.

case_title: Suez Canal blockage and rerouting pressure in 2021
historical_outcome: Shippers first relied on rerouting, schedule visibility, and reserve logistics after the Suez blockage rather than solving the disruption through rapid source substitution.

sources.title: Update to Vessel blockage in the Suez Canal
Navigation in the Suez Canal is back in both directions

intake.event_framing: Assess supply-chain responses after the March 2021 Suez Canal blockage disrupts global shipping schedules.
intake.current_development: The Ever Given blocks the Suez Canal, vessels queue on both sides, and shippers scramble to reroute or absorb schedule delays.
known_constraints: The disruption is in transport, not product design or supplier capability
Freight must keep moving through alternate routes or delayed schedules
known_unknowns: How long the blockage and clearing effort will keep networks out of balance

summary: Operators will prioritize rerouting and reserve logistics over immediate supplier substitution

documents.maersk-suez.md: Maersk said the Suez Canal was blocked in both directions and customers should expect rerouting pressure and schedule disruption.
documents.sca-reopening.md: The Suez Canal Authority later said navigation resumed in both directions after the canal was reopened.

case_title: Red Sea shipping rerouting and Cape diversion in late 2023
historical_outcome: Major carriers responded to the late-2023 Red Sea attacks by pausing transit, rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope, and leaning on logistics contingencies.

sources.title: Maersk Operations through Red Sea / Gulf of Aden
Readout of Secretary of Defense Lloyd J. Austin III's Virtual Conference on Red Sea Maritime Security

intake.event_framing: Assess supply-chain responses after late-2023 Red Sea attacks push carriers toward Cape rerouting.
intake.current_development: Escalating attacks on commercial vessels in the Red Sea push carriers to pause transits and divert around the Cape of Good Hope while maritime security coordination ramps up.
known_constraints: The immediate binding problem is transport-route safety and predictability
Cargo owners need continuity even if transit times lengthen
known_unknowns: Whether security measures will restore enough confidence for normal Red Sea transit to resume quickly

summary: Carriers will favor diversion and contingency logistics rather than wait for perfect route security

documents.maersk-red-sea.md: Maersk said it paused Red Sea transits, diverted around the Cape of Good Hope, and treated that route as the faster and more predictable outcome for customers.
documents.red-sea-security.md: Defense officials said multinational maritime security coordination was needed because attacks on shipping were threatening the global economy.

case_title: Colonial Pipeline ransomware disruption and East Coast fuel stress
historical_outcome: The Colonial Pipeline incident triggered emergency logistics and resilience measures to keep fuel moving rather than a clean supplier-side substitution solution.

sources.title: DHS announces new cybersecurity requirements for critical pipeline owners and operators

intake.event_framing: Assess supply-chain responses after the 2021 Colonial Pipeline ransomware incident disrupts fuel flows on the U.S. East Coast.
intake.current_development: A cyberattack disrupts a major fuel pipeline, creating transport bottlenecks and forcing emergency resilience and logistics measures.
known_constraints: Fuel distribution continuity matters more than supplier substitution in the acute phase
Transport resilience and recovery planning are the immediate bottlenecks
known_unknowns: How quickly operators can restore secure operations without prolonged downstream shortages

summary: The acute response will emphasize resilience, contingency logistics, and restoration of network function

documents.tsa-pipeline.md: TSA said the Colonial Pipeline incident temporarily disrupted critical supplies of gasoline and refined petroleum products along the East Coast.
documents.cybersecurity-directive.md: TSA said new pipeline security directives required operators to implement urgent cybersecurity and resilience measures after the incident.
